sections.paragraph:
ὀψέ [cp. ὀπι-in ὄπισθεν, q.v.] temporal adv., ‘in a period of time that is after another period’

—a. used in reference to end of term of activity normally associated with hours of daylight and therefore indicating relative lateness, late Mk 11:11 v.l. (ὀψὲ ἤδη οὔσης τῆς ὥρας the time being quite late [Jesus had looked around at everything, vs. 11a]); (at) eventide, evening vs. 19; 13:35.

—b. with focus on subsequential aspect, after,strongly indicated by context Mt 28:1.
